ltm.work_method: Orchestrates a multi-agent fleet through role-based subagent dispatch, with dedicated auditors, test builders, debuggers, bug-fix agents, and research scouts. Directs implementation through specification documents that agents must study before coding. Frustrated by persistent bootstrap truncation warnings (~29-37% context loss), signals overload through terse aborts like "try again" and "Push everything and try again". Demands concrete deliverables: commit hashes, test counts, pass/fail status, line-specific references. Uses feature branches (turbovec-integration-ccc) for parallel workstreams. Recently introduced a novel validation pattern: deploying subagents as beta-testers roleplaying external GitHub visitors who discover repos as real-world tools, stress-testing usability and documentation from an outsider perspective.
ltm.communication: Technical, imperative, and throughput-oriented. Communicates almost exclusively through subagent task schematics rather than conversational dialogue — buffered async directives with precise deliverable checklists. Approval remains minimal ("great", "Great", "Awesome"), frustration manifests as terse "try again" or aborted runs. Uses fleet shorthand (CCC, sunset-ecosystem, Cocapn Fleet) assuming contextual fluency. Requests broad strategic thinking wrapped in specific implementation mandates: "research cutting-edge", "think about killer app potential", "deep think about improvements". Recently directed creative reframing of subagent missions — beta-testers as "visitors from the outside who found our repos on github" — showing interest in narrative framing for operational goals.
ltm.temporal: Sunset-ecosystem as active development frontier: BreederDaemonV2 lifecycle FSM, AutoBreeder integration into breeding loop, RoomGridCompiler with hot-swap A/B testing, FluxVectorTable for diversity search, HolonomyConsensus for distributed consensus, MetronomeBridge synchronization, FleetConductor stress testing at 100-room/50-agent scale. Infrastructure hardening: fixing pytest collection hangs, building test_compiler_integration.py with speedup>1.0 and correctness gates. New operational layer: external-visitor beta-testing paradigm for usability validation. Decision journal extension (log_spawn, log_sunset, log_breeder) for operational traceability. Bug-fix operations targeting specific failing tests (test_replay_recover). Cross-repo intelligence tracking Forgemaster's recent commits for integration prioritization.
ltm.taste: Systems architect with game-world sensibility — "Cocapn Fleet", "sunset" ecosystem, "greenhorns" leveling to "Able-bodied crewmen", crabs with multiple claws. Values empirical verification culture: A/B correctness tests, stress thresholds, commit-hash accountability. Recently demonstrated interest in user-experience validation through narrative framing — treating tool usability as a discoverability story worth simulating. Pursues radical performance through hardware-conscious design, speculative low-level language refactoring, and diversity-search algorithms. Appreciates speculative foresight (3-5 year horizon mapping) as legitimate engineering input. Operational aesthetic: sustained overnight autonomy, minimal check-ins, self-healing infrastructure. Balances playful metaphor with hard metrics — "producing gold" means passing tests and clean commits.
